title: craspedia chrysantha
intro: Craspedia chrysantha, also known as Billy Buttons or Australian Cup, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow or white flowers resembling miniature daffodils. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Craspedia chrysantha include relieving coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as easing menstrual cramps and other female reproductive issues. Craspedia chrysantha can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
